content: DSM-5-TR Insomnia Disorder criteria: Predominant complaint of dissatisfaction with sleep quantity/quality with ≥1 symptoms: difficulty initiating sleep, difficulty maintaining sleep (frequent awakenings/trouble returning to sleep), early morning awakening with inability to return to sleep. Causes clinically significant distress/impairment in daytime functioning (fatigue, mood disturbance, cognitive impairment, reduced motivation/energy, increased errors/accidents, tension headaches, GI symptoms, concern about sleep). Sleep difficulty occurs ≥3 nights/week, present for ≥3 months, occurs despite adequate opportunity for sleep. Not better explained by/exclusive to another sleep-wake disorder, substance effects, or co-occurring mental/medical disorders. Specifiers: episodic, persistent, recurrent.
